question: Does your reward function use AI tools for benchmarking or market analysis?
real_anchor: No UK prevalence figure for AI in reward benchmarking specifically
notes: HiBob 31% covers AI in pay decisions (anchored on REW264_GOV_AIPAYREVIEW); this asks about the reward function's own tooling — different construct.

question: Do you operate differentiated reward arrangements for AI/digital talent (e.g. separate ranges, RSUs, retention vehicles)?
real_anchor: No UK-only prevalence for differentiated AI-talent reward
notes: Global consultancy surveys exist but are excluded (US/global base). Co-op-first metric.

question: Have any roles been re-graded or re-evaluated in the last 12 months specifically due to AI changing job content?
real_anchor: No published UK figure for AI-driven re-grading activity
notes: Emerging practice; expect CIPD coverage in future waves.

question: Do you disclose to employees when AI has been used in decisions affecting their pay?
real_anchor: No prevalence data on AI pay-decision disclosure
notes: Regulatory direction of travel (EU AI Act read-across); no UK employer figure yet.

question: If SAYE is offered, what discount is applied to the option price?
real_anchor: Majority of SAYE schemes understood to grant at or near the 20% maximum
source: ProShare annual SAYE/SIP survey; HMRC ESS statistics (480 live SAYE schemes, prior-validated) — VERIFY discount split before seeding
base: ProShare base = responding plan issuers, not all UK employers
notes: Do not seed a discount split until ProShare table verified.

question: If a SIP is offered, which elements are used?
real_anchor: Partnership + matching understood to be the most common SIP pairing
source: ProShare annual survey; HMRC ESS (800 live SIP schemes, prior-validated) — VERIFY element mix
notes: Element-mix split needs ProShare table; scheme counts already validated.

question: What is the approximate employee participation rate in your all-employee share plan?
real_anchor: Typical all-employee participation understood to sit in the 25–40% band
source: ProShare participation tables — VERIFY before seeding
notes: Participation varies strongly by contribution level; seed conservatively pending verification.

question: Is any employee-ownership structure in place or under consideration (e.g. EOT)?
real_anchor: ~2,000+ UK employee-owned businesses, majority via EOT
source: Employee Ownership Association sector data — VERIFY current count and base
base: EOA counts businesses, not share of all UK employers
notes: Prevalence among the general employer population is very low; seed accordingly.

question: Do any incentive plans include ESG, safety or people measures?
real_anchor: ESG measures now common in listed-company exec incentives; all-employee prevalence unpublished
source: CIPD Reward Management archive / PPT tables — VERIFY whether an all-population figure exists
base: Listed-company exec data (e.g. FTSE) would be the wrong base for this question
notes: Do not borrow FTSE exec statistics; question covers any plan including general population.

question: How is discretion applied to formulaic bonus outcomes?
real_anchor: No UK prevalence for bonus-discretion governance models
notes: Practice metric; co-op distribution is the product.

question: Are bonus buy-outs offered to new senior hires?
real_anchor: No general-market UK buy-out prevalence figure
notes: Listed-company disclosure exists for exec hires only; wrong base for this question.

question: When are annual bonuses typically paid relative to year end?
real_anchor: No published distribution of bonus payment timing
notes: Practice metric; timing norms are exactly what the co-op reveals.

question: Do you operate a profit-share or gainshare arrangement for the general population?
real_anchor: Historic CIPD data put all-employee profit share in the low-teens % of employers
source: CIPD Reward Management survey archive — VERIFY latest figure and year
notes: Archive figure may be dated; verify vintage before use.

question: For commission plans, are earnings capped?
real_anchor: No UK-only commission-cap prevalence
notes: US sales-comp surveys excluded by register rule.

question: Do you operate a retention or project-completion bonus practice for critical roles?
real_anchor: No UK prevalence figure for retention-bonus frameworks
notes: CIPD LMO covers counter-offers/retention sentiment, not framework prevalence.

question: What is your typical promotion pay increase policy?
real_anchor: Median UK promotion increases typically reported in the 8–10% region
source: Brightmine (XpertHR) pay-planning research — VERIFY figure, year and base
notes: Paywalled source; verify via published summary before seeding.

question: Do you operate a formal counter-offer policy?

source: CIPD Labour Market Outlook (prior-validated source family) — VERIFY exact wave and figure
notes: LMO asks incidence, not policy stance; note construct gap when verifying.

question: Do you apply geographic pay differentiation within the UK?

notes: Allowance value data exists; structure-type split may not.

question: What is your policy on pay for employees at or above range maximum?

source: Brightmine pay-structure research — VERIFY split
notes: Practice-adjacent market metric; ordering ratified best-first as listed.

question: Are line managers given pay-decision training or guidance before review cycles?

question: How are pay review outcomes communicated to individuals?

question: Do you offer backup or emergency care days (child or adult dependants)?

question: Do you provide paid grandparental leave?

question: Is surrogacy treated with parity to adoption in pay and leave policies?

question: Do you provide paid leave or formal support for employees experiencing domestic abuse?

notes: Membership is commitment, not provision; do not conflate when verifying.
